what_it_does: Defines the npm package metadata, scripts, CLI binaries, dependencies, and devDependencies for recollection, an ESM Node package described as cross-session corpus-wide memory for Claude Code/Codex.
observations: Version is 1.4.2 with a description referencing episodic-memory v1.4.2 refit.
Four bin entries: recollection, recollection-index, recollection-search, and recollection-mcp-server.
Build pipeline: generate-version, tsc, then esbuild bundle of src/mcp-server.ts into dist/mcp-server.js.
Bundle externalizes many native/ML packages: fsevents, @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, sharp, onnxruntime-node, better-sqlite3, sqlite-vec, @huggingface/transformers, proper-lockfile.
Dependencies include @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, @huggingface/transformers, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, better-sqlite3, marked, proper-lockfile, sqlite-vec, zod.
Test scripts use vitest plus claude-e2e and codex-e2e custom scripts.
Repository and homepage are local file URLs pointing to /home/tim/recollection.
standouts: The esbuild bundle externalizes a large set of heavy/native/ML dependencies, including some not listed in dependencies (e.g., sharp, onnxruntime-node).
Two e2e test scripts target Claude and Codex explicitly.
An MCP server binary is exposed alongside indexing and search CLIs.
conventions: Follows standard npm package.json structure (name, version, description, main, types, bin, scripts, dependencies, devDependencies, license, repository, homepage).
Uses npm lifecycle hooks: prebuild, pretest, postinstall.
Uses conventional script names: build, test, test:watch.
Provides TypeScript declaration output via types field.
concerns: Repository and homepage URLs are local file paths, which are non-portable outside the original environment.
Several esbuild externals (sharp, onnxruntime-node) are not listed in dependencies, so their runtime availability is unclear.
No engines field declares supported Node versions despite native dependencies.
postinstall behavior is opaque from this file alone.
notes: This file is the package manifest for recollection, wiring its CLI/MCP entry points, build/test lifecycle, and a mix of agent SDK, vector SQLite, and transformer dependencies.
questions: What does scripts/postinstall.js do at install time?
What file does scripts/generate-version.js produce and how is it consumed?
Why are sharp and onnxruntime-node externalized if they are not declared as dependencies?
What do test:claude-e2e and test:codex-e2e actually exercise?
Are the local repository/homepage URLs intentional for an offline/local-only project?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the file: it configures a package for cross-session conversation memory with binaries for CLI use, indexing, search, and an MCP server, plus build/test infrastructure.
apparent_themes: Tight integration with Claude Code and Codex
Heavy reliance on native and ML runtime modules
intention_signals: The package is trying to be an installable, buildable memory layer that exposes multiple entry points so users can index past conversations, search them semantically, and expose that capability to an MCP-compatible client.
novelty: Explicit externalization of many large native/ML modules in the esbuild bundle, combined with local-only repository/homepage URLs and dedicated Claude/Codex e2e test scripts.
contribution: This file contributes the canonical package surface area: declared entry points, build and test commands, dependency footprint, and versioning hooks that any fused understanding of the recollection system must preserve.
summary_for_embedding: package.json for recollection, an ESM Node package providing CLI and MCP server binaries for cross-session conversation memory, with TypeScript build/esbuild bundling, Vitest tests, and dependencies spanning SQLite vector storage, transformers, and agent SDKs.
intention_for_embedding: Declare and wire the recollection package's entry points, build pipeline, dependency set, and test commands to support indexing, searching, and serving episodic memory for Claude Code and Codex.
